title: Hydro dissection, irrigation, and suction electrocautery devices and methods of use
abstract: A laparoscopic instrument integrates electrocautery forceps with high velocity hydro-dissection, low velocity irrigation, and suction capability. When a sheath containing a distal internal bushing is fully retracted on the forceps shaft, exposing its jaws, the inner lumen of the bushing seals against the outer surface of the forceps shaft. Fluid inside the sheath is directed to a tiny nozzle, forming a high velocity' fluid jet for tissue hydroÂ¬ dissection. An electrode attached to the sheath retraction actuator makes electrical contact with a corresponding electrode in the device handle housing to deliver electrocautery only when the forceps are extended. Magnetic coupling ensures stable electrical contact and provides user haptic feedback regarding full sheath retraction. Distal sheath extension enlarges the flow path between the tapered forceps jaws and the inner lumen of the bushing, providing low velocity fluid irrigation or allowing fluid suction to occur.
claims:
Claims WE CLAIM: 1. A device for performing a surgical procedure within a patientâs body, comprising: a tubular member comprising a proximal end, a distal end, and a lumen extending between the proximal and distal ends; a source of fluid coupled to the lumen to deliver pressurized fluid through the lumen; an instrument comprising a shaft slidably received within the lumen and an end effector on an end of the shaft disposed adjacent the distal end of the tubular member, the shaft sized to accommodate fluid flowing from the source through the lumen around the shaft; a handle comprising a first actuator coupled to the tubular member to move the tubular member between a distal position where the distal end covers the end effector and a proximal position where the end effector is exposed beyond the distal end; and an annular member on the distal end including a primary passage therethrough sized such that, in the distal position, fluid from the source passes from the lumen around the end effector to deliver the fluid out the primary passage, and, in the proximal position, the annular member engages the shaft to seal the primary passage, the annular member comprising a secondary lumen adjacent the primary passage having a cross-section smaller than the primary passage such that the fluid from the source flows through the lumen and out the secondary passage to provide hydro-dissection fluid. 2. The device of claim 1, further comprising a second actuator on the handle for actuating the end effector. 3. The device of claim 2, wherein the end effector comprises a pair of jaws coupled to the second actuator for directing the jaws between open and closed positions. 4. The device of claim 3, wherein the jaws comprise substantially blunt contact surfaces configured to grasp tissue between the jaws in the closed position. 5. The device of claim 4, further comprising a third actuator on the handle configured to activate the source of fluid after the jaws grasp tissue to deliver the 

pressurized fluid through the lumen with the tubular member in the proximal position to perform hydro-dissection. 6. The device of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the source of fluid comprises a pump carried by the handle. 7. The device of claim 6, wherein the source of fluid further comprises a reservoir of fluid coupled to the pump. 8. The device of claim 7, wherein the reservoir comprises a bag of fluid external to the device. 9. The device of claim 8, further comprising tubing coupled between the bag and the pump. 10. The device of any one of claims 1-9, further comprising: a connector on the handle configured to be coupled to a source of vacuum; an actuator for selectively opening a first flow path between the source of pressurized fluid and the lumen and a second path between the connector and the lumen. 11. The device of claim 10, wherein the actuator comprises an actuatable valve configured to selectively open and close the first and second flow paths. 12. The device of claim 11, wherein the valve is biased to a position wherein the first path is open to deliver pressurized fluid to the lumen and the second path is closed. 13. The device of claim 12, wherein the actuator is configured to overcome the bias to close the first path and open the second path to deliver the vacuum through the lumen to provide suction into the primary opening in the annular member with the tubular member in the distal position. 14. The device of any one of claims 1-9, further comprising a cautery element on the end effector, the handle comprising comprises a connector for coupling the cautery 

element to a source of energy to deliver energy to the cautery element to cauterize tissue contacted by the cautery element. 15. The device of claim 14, wherein the cautery element comprises one or more electrodes on the end effector. 16. The device of claim 14, wherein the end effector comprises a pair of jaws and wherein the cautery element comprises one of a conductive surface and an electrode on one of the jaws. 17. The device of claim 14, wherein the source of electrical power comprises a generator configured to be coupled to the connector to deliver electrical energy to the cautery element via the one or more leads to cauterize tissue contacted by the cautery element. 18. The device of claim 14, further comprising a switch configured to open and close a conductive path from the connector to the cautery element. 19. The device of claim 18, wherein the switch is configured to close the conductive path when the tubular member is positioned in the proximal position such that the end effector is fully exposed from the distal end to allow deliver of electrical energy of the cautery element. 20. The device of claim 18, wherein the switch is configured to open the conductive path when the tubular member is moved distally from the proximal position to prevent deliver of electrical energy of the cautery element when the end effector is partially or completed covered by the distal end. 21. The device of claim 18, wherein the first actuator comprises a sliding actuator configured to be manipulated to move the tubular member between the proximal and distal positions, and wherein the switch is coupled to the sliding actuator. 22. The device of claim 18, wherein the switch comprises a stabilizer that stabilizes the switch in positions that open and close the conductive path. 23. The device of claim 22, wherein the stabilizer comprises a magnetic coupling. 24. The device of claim 23, wherein the magnetic coupling is configured to provide haptic feedback to a user when the tubular member is in the proximal position to provide confirmation that the conductive path is closed. 25. The device of claim 18, further comprising a feedback element configured to provide haptic feedback to a user when the tubular member is in the proximal position to provide confirmation that the conductive path is closed. 26. The device of claim 25, wherein the feedback element comprise an element coupled to the first actuator. 27. The device of claim 25, wherein the feedback element is configured to generate a vibration or other tactile feedback when the tubular member is retracted to the proximal position. 28. The device of claim 25, wherein the feedback element is configured to generate a sound when the tubular member is retracted to the proximal position. 29. The device of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the annular member comprises a bushing on the distal end of the tubular member. 30. The device of claim 20, wherein the bushing comprises a distal wall including the primary and secondary passages therethrough. 31. A device for performing a surgical procedure within a patientâs body, comprising: 

 a tubular member comprising a proximal end, a distal end, and a lumen extending between the proximal and distal ends; a source of fluid coupled to the lumen to deliver pressurized fluid through the lumen; an instrument comprising a shaft slidably received within the lumen and an end effector on an end of the shaft disposed adjacent the distal end of the tubular member, the shaft sized to accommodate fluid flowing from the source through the lumen around the shaft; a handle comprising a first actuator coupled to the tubular member to move the tubular member between a distal position where the distal end covers the end effector and a proximal position where the end effector is exposed beyond the distal end; an annular member on the distal end including a primary passage therethrough sized such that, in the distal position, fluid from the source passes from the lumen around the end effector to deliver the fluid out the primary passage, and, in the proximal position, the annular member engages the shaft to seal the primary passage, the annular member comprising a secondary lumen adjacent the primary passage having a cross-section smaller than the primary passage such that the fluid from the source flows through the lumen and out the secondary passage to provide hydro-dissection fluid; a cautery element on the end effector; and a switch configured to open and close a conductive path from a source of electrical energy to the cautery element. 32. The device of claim 31, wherein the switch is configured to automatically close the conductive path when the tubular member is positioned in the proximal position such that the end effector is fully exposed from the distal end to allow deliver of electrical energy of the cautery element. 33. A system for performing a surgical procedure within a patientâs body, comprising: a device according to any one of claims 14-32; and a generator configured to be coupled to the device to deliver electrical energy to the cautery element. 34. A method for performing a surgical procedure within a patientâs body, comprising: providing a laparoscopic device comprising a tubular member comprising a proximal end, a distal end, and a lumen extending between the proximal and distal ends; an instrument comprising a shaft slidably received within the lumen and an end effector on an end of the shaft disposed adjacent the distal end of the tubular member; a handle comprising a first actuator coupled to the tubular member to move the tubular member between a distal position where the distal end covers the end effector and a proximal position where the end effector is exposed beyond the distal end; and an annular member on the distal end including a primary passage therethrough and a secondary lumen adjacent the primary passage having a cross-section smaller than the primary passage; introducing the distal end into a patientâs body; with the tubular member in the proximal position such that the primary passage is sealed against the shaft, delivering pressurized fluid through the lumen such that the fluid flows through the lumen and out the secondary passage to provide hydro-dissection fluid to dissect tissue within the patientâs body; and with the tubular member in the distal position, delivering pressurized fluid through the lumen around the shaft and out the primary passage to provide irrigation fluid. 35. A device for performing a surgical procedure within a patientâs body, comprising: an outer sheath comprising a proximal end, a distal end, and a lumen extending between the proximal and distal ends; an instrument comprising a shaft, an end effector on a distal end of the shaft extending distally from the sheath distal end, the shaft sized to accommodate fluid flowing through the lumen around the shaft; a tubular member slidable on the sheath distal end between a distal position in which a primary passage in a distal tip of the tubular member is disposed distally beyond the sheath distal end and a proximal position in which the primary passage seals against the sheath distal end to prevent flow through the primary passage, the tubular member comprising a secondary lumen secondary lumen extending through the distal tip adjacent the primary passage having a cross-section smaller than the cross-section of the primary passage; 

 a handle on a proximal end of the sheath comprising a first actuator coupled to the tubular member to move the tubular member between the distal and proximal positions; and a source of fluid coupled to the lumen to deliver pressurized fluid through the lumen and out an outlet in the sheath distal end to dissect tissue such that, in the distal position, the fluid flows through the lumen around the shaft and out the primary passage to provide irrigation fluid, and, in the proximal position, the fluid flows through the lumen and out the secondary passage to provide hydro-dissection fluid.
